2605.30590.title: Counterfactual Evaluation Reveals Hidden Capability Profiles in Clinical LLMs and Agents
2605.30590.paper_summary: Two clinical AI systems can score nearly identically on coverage-based rubrics yet behave radically differently when their patient inputs change: one updates its recommendations to match the new clinical signal, while the other produces the same output regardless. We introduce the Causal Sensitivity Score (CSS), a pre-registered interventional metric that mutates oncology tumor-board cases along five clinically meaningful dimensions - biomarker flips, prior-treatment failures, biomarker removals, surgery-status changes, and stage perturbations - and scores whether each model updates its recommendations in the pre-registered correct direction using a {0, 0.5, 1.0} scale. Benchmarked against the Consensus Match Score (CMS), a coverage-based weighted recall metric, six frontier models from three labs evaluated in single-shot inference across 224 cases rank in nearly opposite orders: all six models change rank, the CMS-worst model becomes CSS-best, and one upper-mid CMS model ranks last on CSS. We further surface a universal safety blind spot: every frontier model fails on surgery-status interventions (at most 17.2% CSS on Family D), a finding CMS does not expose. The metric also transfers to tool-using agents: in a ReAct-style experiment, tool use improves CSS for five of six models (+2.5 to +20.3 percentage points), yet the lowest-CSS model retrieves the same chart sections and still fails to update its recommendations - revealing a structural responsiveness deficit visible only under counterfactual evaluation. Cross-judge replication and three-rater medical-professional validation confirm the aggregate findings. Interventional pre-registered metrics like CSS complement coverage-based evaluation for clinical AI agents: they capture responsiveness that coverage metrics miss and offer a candidate dense reward signal for future agentic RL systems.
2605.30590.paper_summary_zh: 兩個臨床 AI 系統在基於覆蓋率的評分標準上幾乎得分相同，但當患者輸入變化時，它們的行為卻截然不同：一個系統根據新的臨床信號更新其建議，而另一個系統則無論如何都產生相同的輸出。我們介紹了因果敏感度分數 (CSS)，這是一個預註冊的干預指標，沿著五個臨床意義重大的維度變更腫瘤委員會案例——生物標記翻轉、先前治療失敗、生物標記移除、手術狀態變更和階段擾動——並評分每個模型是否在預註冊的正確方向上更新其建議，使用 {0, 0.5, 1.0} 的標準。與共識匹配分數 (CMS) 進行基準比較，來自三個實驗室的六個前沿模型在 224 個案例中的單次推理評估中幾乎排名相反：所有六個模型的排名都發生變化，CMS 最差的模型成為 CSS 最佳，而一個中上等的 CMS 模型在 CSS 中排名最後。我們進一步揭示了一個普遍的安全盲點：每個前沿模型在手術狀態干預上都失敗（在 D 類家庭中最多 17.2% CSS），這一發現是 CMS 所未揭示的。該指標還可轉移到使用工具的代理：在 ReAct 風格的實驗中，工具使用提高了六個模型中五個的 CSS（+2.5 到 +20.3 個百分點），然而最低 CSS 的模型檢索相同的圖表部分，仍然未能更新其建議——揭示了只有在反事實評估下才能顯現的結構性反應不足。交叉評審重複和三評審醫療專業驗證確認了總體發現。像 CSS 這樣的干預預註冊指標補充了基於覆蓋率的臨床 AI 代理評估：它們捕捉到覆蓋率指標所忽略的反應性，並為未來的代理強化學習系統提供了一個候選的密集獎勵信號。
2605.30590.author: Matt Turk et.al.
